questions.question: The ancient Egyptians used $x^2 + 10x + 20$ to model the area of a rectangular field. What is the vertex form of this equation?
Complete the square for $x^2 + 7x$ to find the vertex form of the equation.
The ancient Babylonians used $x^2 - 4x - 3$ to calculate the volume of a storage container. Convert this equation to vertex form.
The ancient Greeks used $x^2 + 2x + 1$ to model the trajectory of a projectile. What is the vertex form of this equation?
The vertex form of a quadratic equation is $(x - 3)^2 + 2$. What is the standard form of this equation?
Complete the square for $x^2 - 9x$ to find the vertex form of the equation.
The ancient Chinese used $x^2 + 5x + 6$ to calculate the area of a triangular field. Convert this equation to vertex form.
The vertex form of a quadratic equation is $(x + 2)^2 - 3$. What is the standard form of this equation?
Complete the square to rewrite $x^2 + 12x + 4$ in vertex form. Then, interpret the transformed equation.
The ancient Egyptians used the equation $x^2 - 8x + 15$ to model the height of a pyramid. Convert this equation to vertex form, then sketch the graph of the equation and identify the x-intercepts.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Use the completing the square method to convert standard form to vertex form.
Tip 2: Make sure to simplify the equation after completing the square.
Tip 3: Always identify the vertex and x-intercepts of the equation after transforming it.
